docs.title: Welcome to the PCM Documentation Project
docs.text:
Creating better PCM documentation, one function at a time.

Who is this for? CMM Programmers, Automation Enthusiasts, and Metrology Engineers looking to master the Calypso scripting language.

docs.title: What is PCM?
docs.text:
PCM (Parameter Coded Measurement) is the internal scripting engine used within the Zeiss Calypso software. While Calypso handles the heavy lifting of CMM (Coordinate Measuring Machine) data collection, PCM is used to control the logic, process data, and customize inspection routines.

Whether you are writing a simple script to check tolerances or a complex automation script, this documentation aims to make PCM accessible to everyone.

docs.title: Why use this site?
docs.text:
• For Operators: Clear, simple examples that help you automate repetitive checks.
  • For Engineers: A reference guide for the internal syntax and math functions.
  • For Programmers: An open resource for extending Calypso's capabilities.

docs.title: Get Involved
docs.text: Documentation is never truly finished. If you find a function that works differently than documented, or if you have a snippet that helps your workflow, we want to know.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Name Type Optional Description name type optional desc ... Any Yes Remaining arguments

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Converts a given number from radians to degrees.

Since Calypso X.Y

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Name Type Optional Description number Integer, Double No number to convert from radians to degrees

docs.title: Return Value
docs.text: Integer, Double - degree equivalent of the given value from radians

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Converts a given number from degrees to radians.

Since Calypso X.Y

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Name Type Optional Description number Integer, Double No number to convert from degrees to radians

docs.title: Return Value
docs.text: Integer, Double - radian equivalent of the given value from degrees

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Unofficial command to execute SmallTalk code through PCM scripting.

Since Calypso X.Y

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Name Type Optional Description commands String No SmallTalk code to execute ... Any Yes Remaining arguments.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: • a practical joke by Eric Moberg on the Zeiss Forums

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Name Type Optional Description name type optional desc ... Any Yes Remaining arguments.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Provides a text based date based on input given.

Since Calypso X.Y

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Name Type Optional Description number Integer Yes Only accepts the following values: 0, 1, and 2

docs.title: Return Value
docs.text:
Depending on the number passed to the function, will get the following String returned:

Value Result Example 0 (Default if empty) Full text date January 1, 2026 1 ISO Standard Date Format 2026-01-01 2 Filename safe output 2026_01_01
